summary: Verified the Dashboard greeting bug fix end-to-end. Anonymous /app renders 'Hi Meera! Preserve a memory today.' Signed-in users (fake-seeded Mongo session cookie) render 'Hi <FirstName>! Preserve a memory today.' — validated with a two-word name (Priya Sharma -> 'Hi Priya!') and a single-word name (Karthik -> 'Hi Karthik!'). Backend regressions confirmed: /api/auth/me returns 401 without cookie, 200 with valid Bearer/cookie; /api/recipes and /api/families still return the demo user's Morkuzhambu + Rao Family for anonymous requests and return [] for the seeded user (proving per-user scoping via AuthContextMiddleware).
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.jsx line 67: greeting expression `((authUser?.name || 'Meera').trim().split(' ')[0]) || 'there'` — the trailing `|| 'there'` is unreachable because .split(' ')[0] on a non-empty trimmed string is always truthy. Not a bug (fallback 'Meera' is applied earlier), just dead code. Fine to leave.

Dashboard.jsx and AppShell.jsx both independently call api.authMe() on mount. Consider hoisting the authenticated-user state to a React context/provider to avoid duplicate network calls on every route change.
server.py still >1900 lines and mixes 8+ feature domains. Splitting into routers/modules would reduce merge conflicts (was already flagged in iteration_1).

AuthContextMiddleware silently falls back to 'demo-user' when no valid session is present. This is intentional (documented in code) but means anonymous /app users see demo Meera's real data. If demo data ever includes anything private, this needs revisiting.
test_credentials: No live creds needed. Seeded fake sessions directly into Mongo user_sessions collection; cleaned up after each test.
seed_data_creation: Two test users were temporarily seeded (Priya Sharma + Karthik) with matching user_sessions rows; both users AND sessions were deleted post-test (verified: 2 sessions + 2 users deleted).
context_for_next_testing_agent: New pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_dashboard_greeting_auth.py exercises /api/auth/me + data scoping using pymongo to seed users. It self-cleans via fixture teardown. Playwright can set httpOnly cookies via context.add_cookies({...httpOnly:True, sameSite:'None', secure:True}) on the preview host — verified working. Backend supports Authorization: Bearer <session_token> as an alternative to the cookie.
rca of the issue: Original bug: greeting was hardcoded 'Hi Meera!' regardless of the signed-in user. Fix (already applied by main agent before this iteration): (1) Dashboard.jsx now fetches api.authMe() in a useEffect and stores authUser state. (2) The h1 renders 'Hi ${authUser?.name?.split(' ')[0] || 'Meera'}! Preserve a memory today.' — first word of the name, with 'Meera' fallback only when authUser is null. (3) api.authMe() calls GET /api/auth/me with withCredentials:true so the httpOnly session cookie is sent. All acceptance criteria in the review request pass.
